title: pleiotaxis lawalreana
intro: Pleiotaxis lawalreana, also known as the Lawalreea's Pleiotaxis, is a plant species in the daisy family (Asteraceae) that is native to the tropical regions of Africa. Traditionally, the plant has been used in folk medicine, particularly in West Africa, for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, treating wounds and skin conditions, and alleviating digestive issues. The herbal preparations of Pleiotaxis lawalreana typically involve making a tea from the leaves or roots, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
